error.signature: AWS bill for hundreds of dollars after 'free tier' usage due to hidden limits or expiration

verdict.summary: AWS free tier has three types: 12-month free (expires), always free (with limits), and trials. EC2 t2.micro is 12-month only. Leaving resources running after 12 months = full price. Some services have hidden data transfer costs. EBS volumes are not free.

dead_ends.action: Assume all AWS free tier services are perpetually free
dead_ends.why_fails: 12-month free tier services (EC2, RDS, S3) switch to full pricing after 12 months with no warning. You must actively track your free tier anniversary.

dead_ends.action: Spin up resources and forget about them since they're free
dead_ends.why_fails: Free tier has strict limits (750 hrs/month for EC2). Running 2 t2.micro instances exceeds the limit and incurs charges immediately.

dead_ends.action: Only check EC2 costs, ignore other services
dead_ends.why_fails: Data transfer, EBS volumes, Elastic IPs (when not attached), CloudWatch, and Route 53 queries all have separate costs that add up.

workarounds.action: Set up AWS Budgets with alerts at $1, $5, $10 thresholds
workarounds.how: AWS Console -> Budgets -> Create budget -> $5/month threshold -> Email alert

workarounds.action: Use AWS Cost Explorer daily and tag all resources
workarounds.how: Tag everything with project/owner. Use Cost Explorer to spot unexpected charges within 24 hours.

workarounds.action: Enable AWS Organizations SCPs to restrict resource creation in free tier accounts
workarounds.how: Prevent creation of expensive resource types (GPU instances, large RDS) via Service Control Policies
